title: BPP ② validate response
description: Provider-neutral response for resolving an opaque key into identity, capabilities, billing account, and an optional provider-issued signer session. Exercised by NaaP through the BillingProviderAdapter. MUST NOT leak provider-internal metering identifiers (see provider-internal-openmeter.schema.json).

valid.description: Whether the presented key is valid.

sub.description: Stable subject identifier for the resolved user.

capabilities.description: Either the wildcard ["*"] or a list of generic capability ids in the form <pipeline>:<model> or tool:<name>.

quota.description: Remaining quota, or null when not metered/unbounded.

subscriptionRef.description: OPTIONAL neutral opaque subscription pointer. The provider decides its meaning; it MUST NOT encode a provider-internal identifier name (e.g. openmeter_subscription_id).

signerSession.description: OPTIONAL provider-issued signer session, opaque to apps. A provider returns EXACTLY ONE of two neutral forms: (a) a fetchable endpoint {url, headers}, or (b) a token bundle {accessToken, tokenType?, expiresIn?, scope?} (the shape the BillingProviderAdapter / NAAP-C front door return). Each form forbids additional properties, so the two are mutually exclusive.
